preamble: The alveolar nasal /n/ can undergo vocalization, transforming into a nasalized close-mid back rounded vowel /õ/ in specific phonetic environments, particularly within sandhi rules in certain languages.
phoneticEffects: Vocalization of an alveolar nasal consonant, resulting in a nasalized vowel

examples.note: An internal sandhi rule where /n/ is preceded by /r/, /u/, or /e/ and immediately followed by a vowel, semivowel, or nasal, provided no palatal, retroflex, or dental consonant intervenes.

sources: PPT - Sandhi 1 PowerPoint Presentation, free download - ID:786086 - SlideServe. (September 14, 2012).
Part 8 - The Phonetic change of 'm' and 'n' in different positions. (May 02, 2025).
